final_answer.markdown:
No requested claim was verified from the available corpus evidence.

Unresolved from the available corpus evidence:
- Identify the most foundational evaluation/survey papers that anchor the corpus (LLM evaluation, agent evaluation, multimodal evaluation, hallucination, conversation analysis) and briefly state why each is a foundational pick.
- Identify foundational LLM-agent benchmark papers across domains (general agents, software, web, multimodal-visual, rule-guided, games, real-world company tasks, creative/escape, rewards) and explain what each contributes.
- Identify foundational methodology/framework papers for LLM-agent research (agent evaluation methodology, multi-agent frameworks, OpenAI o1 evaluation, world-model decision making, writing testbed, BIGGen bench, BenchAgents, AI Agents That Matter).
- Identify notable social-cognition / writing / self-reasoning evaluation papers and explain what makes each important for evaluating higher-order LLM capabilities.

constraints.coverage_assumption: 'Important' is interpreted as foundational / high-influence papers within the corpus themes on LLM agents and LLM evaluation, since no external citation metrics are exposed by tools. Recommendations are grounded only in titles and themes visible in the 30-paper inventory.
constraints.criteria: foundational/high-influence within corpus themes on LLM agents and LLM evaluation

intent_frame.normalized_question: Recommend important/foundational papers from the 30-paper LLM-agents-and-evaluation corpus, grouped by theme (surveys, agent benchmarks, methodology/frameworks, social-cognition/reasoning evaluations), grounded in titles and themes.

obligations.description: Identify the most foundational evaluation/survey papers that anchor the corpus (LLM evaluation, agent evaluation, multimodal evaluation, hallucination, conversation analysis) and briefly state why each is a foundational pick.

Identify foundational LLM-agent benchmark papers across domains (general agents, software, web, multimodal-visual, rule-guided, games, real-world company tasks, creative/escape, rewards) and explain what each contributes.

Identify foundational methodology/framework papers for LLM-agent research (agent evaluation methodology, multi-agent frameworks, OpenAI o1 evaluation, world-model decision making, writing testbed, BIGGen bench, BenchAgents, AI Agents That Matter).
Identify notable social-cognition / writing / self-reasoning evaluation papers and explain what makes each important for evaluating higher-order LLM capabilities.
